DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFFS GERTRUDIS PANIAGUA, GERTRUDIS PANIAGUA AND ANDRES PANIAGUA'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET TWO IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS: NOT LATER THAN AUGUST 14, 2019 DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HEALTH CARE AND REHAB. INC. SHALL PROVIDE AMENDED RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES 29-34. THE RESPONSES WILL BE TREATED AS CONFIDENTIAL, USED ONLY FOR PURPOSES OF THE INSTANT LITIGATION, NOT DISCLOSED TO ANY THIRD PARTIES EXCEPT AS NECESSARY IN CONNECTION WITH THE LITIGATION (E.G. TO RETAINED CONSULTANTS OR EXPERT WITNESSES, COURT REPORTERS, ETC.), TREATED IN ACCORDANCE WITH RULES OF COURT 2.550 & 2.551, AND FILED UNDER SEAL IF SUBMITTED IN CONNECTION WITH ANY FUTURE DISCOVERY MOTION. THE SCOPE OF DISCOVERY IS BROAD. IT IS NOT PLAINTIFF'S BURDEN TO SHOW THAT ITS INTERROGATORIES SEEK "RELEVANT, DISCOVERABLE INFORMATION"; RATHER, IT IS DEFENDANT'S BURDEN TO JUSTIFY ITS "OBJECTION[S] AND FAILURE TO RESPOND." WILLIAMS V. SUP CT, 3 CAL. 5TH 531, 541 (2017). ON THE LIMITED RECORD ON THIS MOTION, IT IS NOT AT ALL CLEAR THAT THE INFORMATION SOUGHT IS RELEVANT OR WOULD BE ADMISSIBLE FOR ANY PURPOSE, BUT THAT IS A DETERMINATION THAT CAN BE BETTER MADE WITH FULL INFORMATION AVAILABLE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER EMAIL TO RMEAD@FOLGERLEVIN.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM ROGER MEAD, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED. (D302)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-04-2019 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Nov-05-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant San Francisco Health Care And Rehab Inc.'S Motion To File Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-31-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. STEPHEN F. O'NEILL, ESQ. of Tarkington, O'Neill, Barrack & Chong (Tel: 707-576-1380) appearing via CourtCall for the plaintiff;
Atty. LAURA E. MALKOSKY, ESQ. of Ericksen Arbuthnot (Tel: 408-286-0880) appearing for defendants San Francisco Health Care and Rehab Inc. and Marlen Argueda.

HEARING RE: Defendants San Francisco Health Care And Rehab Inc. and Marlen Argueda's Motion To File Cross-Complaint.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants San Francisco Health Care Rehab and Rehab, Inc. and Marlen Arguedas's motion for leave to file cross-complaint is denied. Although leave to file a cross-complaint generally will be liberally granted, Defendants have unduly delayed in seeking leave to add a new party shortly before trial, as the record shows that they were aware in December 2017 of the alleged manufacturing or design defect, yet did not file the application for leave to file a cross-complaint until July 19, 2019. Plaintiff will be prejudiced if, as appears almost inevitable, the addition of a new party will require additional discovery and a continuance of the trial date, which was set on January 30, 2019. Further, if Defendants suffer an adverse verdict, they can bring a separate action for indemnity.

Prevailing party to e-mail a proposed order to the Court today.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185, E-MAIL: tonyvaughn@yahoo.com (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Response To Special Interrogatories, Set Two; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Margaret S. Flynn [Ccp 2030.300
Off Calendar Jul-31-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances: The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFFS GERTRUDIS PANIAGUA, GERTRUDIS PANIAGUA and ANDRES PANIAGUA's Motion To Compel Further Response To Special Interrogatories, Set Two is Granted as follows: Not later than August 14, 2019 Defendant San Francisco Health Care and Rehab. Inc. shall provide amended responses to Plaintiff's Special Interrogatories 29-34. The responses will be treated as confidential, used only for purposes of the instant litigation, not disclosed to any third parties except as necessary in connection with the litigation (e.g. to retained consultants or expert witnesses, court reporters, etc.), treated in accordance with Rules of Court 2.550 & 2.551, and filed under seal if submitted in connection with any future discovery motion. The scope of discovery is broad. It is not plaintiff's burden to show that its interrogatories seek "relevant, discoverable information"; rather, it is defendant's burden to justify its "objection[s] and failure to respond." Williams v. Sup Ct, 3 Cal. 5th 531, 541 (2017). On the limited record on this motion, it is not at all clear that the information sought is relevant or would be admissible for any purpose, but that is a determination that can be better made with full information available. Prevailing party to prepare order email to rmead@folgerlevin.com. Judge Pro Tem Roger Mead, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D302)
